spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the feature's general idea but doesn't surface the named spec API symbols directly.

interactive.rationale: Real controls + script-driven side-effects make this hands-on, not a static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Page covers the promises the feature index makes for this concept.

edge_cases.rationale: Page handles several edge cases (errors, fallbacks, feature detection, denials) — not just the happy path.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link as required.

design_system.rationale: Has 1 raw hex colour value(s) inline — should use --bg-*/--text-*/--accent-* tokens instead.

openQuestions.title: Surface more named spec API for local-network-access-restrictions-for-webtransport
openQuestions.detail: The spec summary lists API surfaces this concept doesn't currently name on-page. Either rename the controls / readouts to mention them, or add a second concept page that targets the missing surface directly. The reviewer extracted these from the spec: Local Network Access for WebTransport. WebTransport connect uses fetch at step 6, inheriting LNA checks. Public→local/loopback prompts user. Deny → network error. No targetAddressSpace equivalent. Mixed-content doesn't apply to webtransport schemes the same way. Same DNS-rebinding protection.

openQuestions.title: Tighten the design-system compliance
openQuestions.detail: Either replace raw hex with --bg-*/--text-*/--accent-* tokens, or pair every button background override with a color override (the global button rule sets both — overriding one alone risks light-on-light).

summary: Workable concept with a couple of gaps. 2 open question(s) queued.
